description: Make the normal user and plugin developer code paths runnable on Node.js without Bun. Only platform contributors (dev mode, tests, build scripts) require Bun. The published npm packages (CLI, MCP server, plugin-tools, create-plugin) must run on Node.js 20+. This means replacing all Bun-specific APIs (Bun.file, Bun.write, Bun.serve, Bun.spawn, Bun.env, Bun.argv, Bun.build, Bun.resolveSync, Bun.CryptoHasher) with Node.js equivalents in production code paths, while keeping bun --hot and bun:test for platform contributor workflows only.

userStories.title: Create a runtime compatibility layer in platform/shared
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want a thin runtime abstraction module in platform/shared that wraps file I/O, process spawning, env vars, and crypto so that production code uses a single import that works on both Bun and Node.js.
acceptanceCriteria: New file platform/shared/src/runtime.ts exports: readFile(path) -> Promise<string>, readJsonFile(path) -> Promise<unknown>, writeFile(path, content) -> Promise<void>, fileExists(path) -> Promise<boolean>, deleteFile(path) -> Promise<void>, spawnProcess(cmd, args, opts) -> {exitCode, stdout, stderr}, spawnProcessSync(cmd, args, opts) -> {exitCode, stdout, stderr}, getEnv(name) -> string | undefined, getArgv() -> string[], sha256(data) -> string

Implementation detects runtime: if typeof Bun !== 'undefined' uses Bun APIs, otherwise uses Node.js equivalents (fs/promises readFile/writeFile/unlink/stat, child_process spawn/spawnSync, process.env, process.argv, crypto.createHash)
atomicWrite in cross-platform.ts is updated to use the new writeFile from runtime.ts instead of Bun.write
platformExec in cross-platform.ts is updated: the bun/bunx case should use 'node'/'npx' when not running under Bun, and the comment is updated to reflect multi-runtime support
getConfigDir in constants.ts uses getEnv from runtime.ts instead of Bun.env
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the foundation story. All subsequent stories depend on importing from this module.

Key files to create/modify:
- platform/shared/src/runtime.ts (NEW) — the compatibility layer
- platform/shared/src/cross-platform.ts — update atomicWrite to use runtime.writeFile, update platformExec comments
- platform/shared/src/constants.ts — update getConfigDir to use runtime.getEnv
- platform/shared/src/index.ts — export the new runtime module

For the runtime detection pattern, use:
```typescript
const isBun = typeof Bun !== 'undefined';
```

For Node.js file I/O:
- readFile: `import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises'` with `encoding: 'utf-8'`
- writeFile: `import { writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises'`
- fileExists: `import { stat } from 'node:fs/promises'` with try/catch
- deleteFile: `import { unlink } from 'node:fs/promises'`
- readJsonFile: readFile + JSON.parse

For Node.js process spawning:
- spawnSync: `import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process'`
- spawn (async): `import { spawn } from 'node:child_process'` — wrap in a promise that resolves when the process exits, collecting stdout/stderr

For crypto:
- sha256: `import { createHash } from 'node:crypto'` → createHash('sha256').update(data).digest('hex')

IMPORTANT: The Bun paths should use the actual Bun APIs (Bun.file().text(), Bun.write(), etc.) for performance when running under Bun. The Node.js paths are the fallback.

Do NOT change any consumers yet (that's for later stories). Only create the layer and update the files within platform/shared.

Make sure to add runtime.ts to the appropriate tsconfig so it's type-checked.

userStories.title: Migrate platform/mcp-server production code to use runtime layer
userStories.description: As a normal user, I want the MCP server to run on Node.js so that I don't need to install Bun to use OpenTabs.
acceptanceCriteria: All Bun.file() calls in mcp-server production code (loader.ts, config.ts, file-watcher.ts, resolver.ts, plugin-management.ts, adapter-files.ts) are replaced with runtime.readFile/readJsonFile/fileExists/deleteFile
All Bun.env references in mcp-server (index.ts, dev-mode.ts, skip-npm-discovery.ts, logger.ts) are replaced with runtime.getEnv
All Bun.spawnSync calls in resolver.ts are replaced with runtime.spawnProcessSync
Bun.spawn call in plugin-management.ts is replaced with runtime.spawnProcess
Bun.resolveSync in resolver.ts is replaced with require.resolve (works in both runtimes) or a try/catch with import.meta.resolve
Bun.argv in dev-mode.ts is replaced with runtime.getArgv
Comments referencing 'bun --hot' or 'Bun.serve()' in production files are updated to say 'hot reload' generically, noting it's a dev-mode feature
The error message in index.ts ('PORT=<number> bun dev') is updated to a generic message
The error message in loader.ts ('cd ${dir} && bun install && bun run build') is changed to 'cd ${dir} && npm install && npm run build'
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the largest story — the MCP server has the most Bun API call sites.

Files to modify (in platform/mcp-server/src/):
- loader.ts: ~4 Bun.file calls (lines 395, 423, 443, 501)
- config.ts: ~2 Bun.file calls (lines 269, 382). Also line 81 if present.
- file-watcher.ts: ~2 Bun.file calls (lines 66, 79)
- resolver.ts: Bun.resolveSync (line 91), 2x Bun.spawnSync (lines 160, 171), Bun.file (line 194). For Bun.resolveSync, use `require.resolve(`${specifier}/package.json`, { paths: [process.cwd()] })` wrapped in try/catch — this works in both Node.js and Bun.
- plugin-management.ts: Bun.spawn (line 84), 2x Bun.file (lines 347, 389)
- adapter-files.ts: 3x Bun.file().delete() (lines 106, 169, 193)
- index.ts: Bun.env.PORT (line 178). NOTE: Do NOT change the Bun.serve() call or HotState/globalThis pattern — those are the frozen shell that only runs under Bun in dev mode. Only change the PORT env var read.
- dev-mode.ts: Bun.argv and Bun.env (line 13)
- skip-npm-discovery.ts: Bun.env (line 12)
- logger.ts: Bun.env (line 61)

For resolver.ts globalThis caching: the caching pattern using globalThis keys (__opentabs_global_paths__) should be kept — it works in both runtimes. Just update the comments to say 'persisted across process lifetime' instead of 'across bun --hot reloads' since it serves both purposes.

Do NOT modify index.ts's Bun.serve() block, HotState interface, or the frozen shell architecture. That code is only executed when running under Bun and is the platform-contributor hot-reload path. The server creation function (createHttpServer) stays Bun-specific — it will be addressed in a later story.

Do NOT modify http-routes.ts — the BunServer interface and Bun.serve()-related types stay because they're part of the server creation path.

userStories.title: Create a Node.js-compatible HTTP+WebSocket server adapter
userStories.description: As a normal user running on Node.js, I want the MCP server to start an HTTP+WebSocket server using node:http and the ws library so that I don't need Bun.
acceptanceCriteria: New file platform/mcp-server/src/server-node.ts creates an HTTP+WebSocket server using node:http and the ws package that matches the same interface as the Bun.serve() delegate shell
The ws package is added as a dependency to platform/mcp-server/package.json
index.ts is refactored: the createHttpServer function detects runtime and calls Bun.serve() under Bun or the Node.js adapter under Node.js
The Node.js server supports the same fetch handler signature (Request => Response), WebSocket upgrade with open/message/close callbacks, and server.port for the actual bound port
The BunServer interface in http-routes.ts is renamed to ServerAdapter (or similar) and works for both runtimes
The HotState.server field type is updated to accept either runtime's server type
Production mode (opentabs start) works correctly on Node.js — HTTP endpoints and WebSocket connections function
Dev mode (bun --hot) continues to work exactly as before under Bun
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the most architecturally critical story. The Bun.serve() pattern must be preserved for hot reload while adding a Node.js alternative.

Approach:
1. Create platform/mcp-server/src/server-node.ts that exports a function matching the shape of Bun.serve() but using node:http + ws.
2. The function should return an object with at minimum: { port: number, stop(): void } plus whatever the delegate shell needs.
3. In index.ts, the createHttpServer function (around line 189) should detect `typeof Bun !== 'undefined'` and branch.
4. The ws package needs to be added: in platform/mcp-server/package.json add `"ws": "^8.18.0"` to dependencies and `"@types/ws": "^8.5.0"` to devDependencies.

For the Node.js server adapter:
- Use `http.createServer()` for the HTTP server
- The fetch handler in http-routes.ts uses Web Standard Request/Response — on Node.js, convert IncomingMessage to Request and send Response back via ServerResponse. Node.js 20+ has global Request/Response via undici.
- Use `new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })` from the ws package and handle the 'upgrade' event on the HTTP server.
- The WebSocket message/close/open callbacks should match the same interface the handlers expect.

For the BunServer interface rename:
- http-routes.ts line 192-196: rename `BunServer` to `ServerAdapter` and update all references.
- The `upgrade` and `timeout` methods need Node.js equivalents. For `timeout`, on Node.js use `res.setTimeout()`. For `upgrade`, the Node.js ws library has its own upgrade mechanism.

IMPORTANT: The Bun.serve() path in index.ts must remain EXACTLY as-is for the hot-reload use case. The Node.js path is a separate code branch, not a replacement.

The http-routes.ts `createHandleFetch` and `createHandleWs*` functions take the server adapter as a parameter — they should work with both runtimes since they use standard Request/Response types.

Also update platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts line 390 comment about 'Bun's per-connection idle timeout' to be generic.

userStories.title: Migrate platform/cli to use runtime layer
userStories.description: As a normal user, I want the opentabs CLI to run on Node.js so that I can install and use it via npm without Bun.
acceptanceCriteria: All Bun.file() calls in CLI source files (cli.ts, config.ts, scaffold.ts, and all commands/*.ts) are replaced with runtime equivalents
All Bun.spawn/Bun.spawnSync calls in CLI (start.ts, plugin.ts, update.ts) are replaced with runtime equivalents
All Bun.env references in CLI (parse-port.ts) are replaced with runtime.getEnv
Bun.write calls in scaffold.ts are replaced with runtime.writeFile
Bun.version reference in doctor.ts is replaced with a runtime-aware check (show Node.js version when not on Bun, show Bun version when on Bun)
The shebang in cli.ts is changed from #!/usr/bin/env bun to #!/usr/bin/env node
start.ts spawns the server with 'node' instead of 'bun' when not running under Bun (uses runtime detection)
update.ts uses 'npm install -g' instead of 'bun install -g' for self-update
The error message in start.ts ('Run bun run build...') is changed to 'Run npm run build...'
The scaffold.ts generated README and console output use npm commands instead of bun commands
The .gitignore template in scaffold.ts includes both bun.lock and package-lock.json
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify in platform/cli/src/:

1. cli.ts (line 1): Change shebang from `#!/usr/bin/env bun` to `#!/usr/bin/env node`. Line 19: Bun.file → runtime.readFile + JSON.parse.

2. commands/start.ts:
   - Line 133: Bun.file().exists() → runtime.fileExists()
   - Line 135: Error message 'Run bun run build...' → 'Run npm run build...'
   - Line 180: `Bun.spawn([platformExec('bun'), serverEntry], ...)` → detect runtime: if Bun, keep as-is; if Node, use `spawn('node', [serverEntry], ...)` from node:child_process. The Bun.spawn result has `.exited` promise and `.stdout`/`.stderr` ReadableStreams; Node's spawn returns a ChildProcess with stream events. Use runtime.spawnProcess or a dedicated helper.

3. commands/plugin.ts:
   - Line 97: Bun.file → runtime.readFile
   - Lines 171, 238, 256, 469: Bun.spawn → runtime.spawnProcess (all npm subcommands)
   - Lines 204, 435, 449: Bun.file → runtime.readFile/readJsonFile

4. commands/update.ts:
   - Line 6-7: Update doc comment from 'bun install -g' to 'npm install -g'
   - Line 26: Bun.file → runtime.readFile + JSON.parse
   - Line 32: Bun.spawnSync → runtime.spawnProcessSync
   - Line 54: JSDoc 'bun install -g' → 'npm install -g'
   - Line 57: `Bun.spawnSync(['bun', 'install', '-g', target])` → `runtime.spawnProcessSync('npm', ['install', '-g', target])`
   - Line 103: Error message 'Try manually: bun install -g...' → 'Try manually: npm install -g...'

5. commands/config.ts (line 351): Bun.file → runtime.readFile

6. commands/doctor.ts:
   - Line 43-48: checkBunVersion function. Rename to checkRuntime. If typeof Bun !== 'undefined', report Bun version. Otherwise report Node.js version from process.version. Both should be 'pass'. Only fail if neither is detected (shouldn't happen).
   - Lines 131, 142, 231, 299: Bun.file → runtime.readFile/fileExists

7. commands/audit.ts (line 127): Bun.file → runtime
8. commands/logs.ts (line 45): Bun.file → runtime
9. commands/setup.ts (lines 24, 49, 50, 60, 83): Bun.file/Bun.write → runtime

10. config.ts (lines 23, 66): Bun.file → runtime
11. parse-port.ts (line 26): Bun.env → runtime.getEnv
12. scaffold.ts:
    - Line 63: Bun.file → runtime
    - Lines 193: .gitignore template — add package-lock.json alongside bun.lock
    - Lines 270-274: README template — change bun commands to npm commands
    - Lines 480-501: Bun.write calls → runtime.writeFile
    - Lines 509-510: Console output — change 'bun install'/'bun run build' to 'npm install'/'npm run build'

userStories.title: Migrate platform/plugin-tools to use esbuild instead of Bun.build
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want opentabs-plugin build to work on Node.js so that I don't need to install Bun to develop plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: esbuild is added as a dependency to platform/plugin-tools/package.json
The bundleIIFE function in build.ts uses esbuild instead of Bun.build for IIFE bundling
All Bun.file/Bun.write calls in build.ts and inspect.ts are replaced with runtime equivalents
Bun.CryptoHasher in build.ts is replaced with runtime.sha256
Bun.env in build.ts is replaced with runtime.getEnv
Bun.spawnSync (tsc fallback) in build.ts is replaced with runtime.spawnProcessSync
The shebang in cli.ts is changed from #!/usr/bin/env bun to #!/usr/bin/env node
Bun.file in cli.ts is replaced with runtime equivalent
The generated IIFE output is identical in behavior (same format, external source map, browser target)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify in platform/plugin-tools/src/:

1. cli.ts:
   - Line 1: Shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bun` → `#!/usr/bin/env node`
   - Line 9: Bun.file → runtime.readFile + JSON.parse

2. commands/build.ts:
   - Lines 117, 131: Bun.file → runtime.readFile/fileExists
   - Line 168: Bun.file → runtime.fileExists
   - Line 184: Bun.env → runtime.getEnv
   - Lines 421-422, 438, 448: Bun.file → runtime.readFile/fileExists
   - Line 560: Bun.file → runtime
   - Line 768: Bun.write → runtime.writeFile
   - Lines 771-780: Replace Bun.build() with esbuild:
     ```typescript
     import { build } from 'esbuild';
     await build({
       entryPoints: [wrapperPath],
       outdir: outDir,
       format: 'iife',
       platform: 'browser',
       bundle: true,
       minify: false,
       sourcemap: 'external',
       outExtension: { '.js': '' },  // need to match ADAPTER_FILENAME
     });
     ```
     NOTE: esbuild's IIFE format may name the output differently. Ensure the output file is named `adapter.iife.js` (ADAPTER_FILENAME). esbuild uses `entryNames` for naming; you may need to rename the output file after build, or use the `outfile` option instead of `outdir`.
   - Line 788: Bun.file().delete() → runtime.deleteFile
   - Lines 818, 839-840, 853: Bun.file → runtime
   - Line 847: Bun.spawnSync → runtime.spawnProcessSync
   - Line 900: Bun.file → runtime.readFile
   - Line 901: `new Bun.CryptoHasher('sha256')` → runtime.sha256
   - Lines 916-917: Bun.write/Bun.file → runtime
   - Line 926: Bun.file → runtime
   - Line 942: Bun.write → runtime.writeFile

3. commands/inspect.ts:
   - Lines 48, 84: Bun.file → runtime

Add esbuild to platform/plugin-tools/package.json:
```json
"dependencies": {
  "esbuild": "^0.24.0"
}
```

IMPORTANT: Test that the esbuild output is functionally identical to Bun.build output. The IIFE wrapper structure, source map references, and browser compatibility must match. Run `opentabs-plugin build` on the e2e-test plugin and verify the adapter.iife.js is valid.

userStories.title: Update package.json files: engines, scripts, and dependencies
userStories.description: As a normal user installing via npm, I want the package.json engine requirements to list Node.js (not Bun) so that npm install works without warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Root package.json engines field adds 'node': '>= 20.0.0' alongside the existing 'bun' requirement (bun stays for platform contributors)
platform/mcp-server/package.json start script is changed from 'bun dist/index.js' to 'node dist/index.js'
platform/mcp-server/package.json dev script keeps 'bun --hot dist/index.js --dev' (platform contributor only)
All platform/*/package.json that have bun-types in devDependencies keep them (needed for type-checking under Bun in dev)
The scripts.check-bun-version postinstall hook in root package.json is updated to only warn (not error) when Bun is missing, since normal users use Node.js
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:

1. Root package.json:
   - engines field: add `"node": ">= 20.0.0"` alongside existing `"bun": ">= 1.3.9"`
   - The postinstall script runs `bun scripts/check-bun-version.ts`. This will fail for Node.js users. Either: (a) make the postinstall conditional on Bun being available, or (b) remove the postinstall hook and rely on the doctor command instead. Recommend (a): change to `"postinstall": "node -e \"\""` or remove it entirely — normal users install via npm and don't need Bun version checks.

2. platform/mcp-server/package.json:
   - Line 17: `"start": "bun dist/index.js"` → `"start": "node dist/index.js"`
   - Line 16: `"dev": "bun --hot dist/index.js --dev"` — KEEP AS-IS (platform contributor only)

3. scripts/check-bun-version.ts: Consider making this fail gracefully if not running under Bun. Add `if (typeof Bun === 'undefined') { console.log('Bun not detected — using Node.js runtime.'); process.exit(0); }` at the top.

4. Do NOT remove bun-types from devDependencies — platform contributors still use Bun for tests and dev mode. The types are needed for type-checking test files that import from bun:test and for the Bun API branches in runtime.ts.

userStories.title: Update browser-extension build scripts to use esbuild
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the extension build scripts to use esbuild so that the build pipeline works on Node.js too, enabling CI without Bun.
acceptanceCriteria: build-extension.ts uses esbuild instead of Bun.build for bundling background.js and offscreen/index.js
build-side-panel.ts uses esbuild instead of Bun.build for bundling the React side panel
Bun.write in build-extension.ts is replaced with Node.js fs writeFile
Bun.file().delete() in build-side-panel.ts is replaced with Node.js fs unlink
esbuild is added as a devDependency to platform/browser-extension/package.json
The package.json scripts that invoke these build scripts use 'node' or 'tsx' instead of 'bun run'
The built extension output (background.js, offscreen/index.js, side-panel.js) is functionally identical
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:

1. platform/browser-extension/build-extension.ts:
   - Replace `Bun.build()` (line 28) with esbuild's `build()` function.
   - esbuild equivalent:
     ```typescript
     import { build } from 'esbuild';
     await build({
       entryPoints: [entrypoint],
       outfile: outfile,
       bundle: true,
       platform: 'browser',
       format: 'esm',
       minify: false,
     });
     ```
   - Replace `Bun.write(outfile, output)` (line 57) — with esbuild's `outfile` option, the output is written directly, no manual write needed.

2. platform/browser-extension/build-side-panel.ts:
   - Replace `Bun.file().delete()` (line 12) with `unlink()` from node:fs/promises (with .catch(() => {}))
   - Replace `Bun.build()` (line 16) with esbuild:
     ```typescript
     await build({
       entryPoints: [join(base, 'src/side-panel/index.tsx')],
       outfile: join(outdir, 'side-panel.js'),
       bundle: true,
       platform: 'browser',
       format: 'esm',
       minify: false,
       define: { 'process.env.NODE_ENV': '"production"' },
     });
     ```

3. platform/browser-extension/package.json:
   - Add `"esbuild": "^0.24.0"` to devDependencies
   - Update build scripts: `"build:bundle": "node build-extension.ts"` → actually these are .ts files, so either: (a) keep using bun to run them in dev, or (b) compile them first. Since these only run during build (which platform contributors do), it's acceptable to keep `bun run build-extension.ts`. BUT the root `bun run build` script chains these, so we need them to work. The simplest approach: keep using `bun` for these build scripts since they're in the contributor workflow. Alternatively, use tsx (TypeScript execution for Node.js).
   - DECISION: Keep build scripts using `bun run` since they're part of the monorepo build that contributors run. The key insight is that building the platform requires Bun, but running the published packages does not.

NOTE: Even though these build scripts are contributor-only, migrating them to esbuild means CI could potentially run on Node.js in the future. However, don't overcomplicate — if keeping Bun for build scripts is simpler, that's fine. The goal is that PUBLISHED artifacts (what npm installs) run on Node.js.

Actually, on reflection: these build scripts ARE contributor-only. Normal users install pre-built packages from npm. Plugin developers run `opentabs-plugin build` (already migrated in US-005). So these can stay Bun-based. BUT — migrating to esbuild here is still valuable because it removes Bun.build() usage, making the codebase more consistent. Do it.

Also update the `build:tailwind` script in package.json which uses `bunx @tailwindcss/cli` — change to `npx @tailwindcss/cli` since npx is more universal.

userStories.title: Update all CLAUDE.md files to reflect dual-runtime support

acceptanceCriteria: Root CLAUDE.md: 'Runtime: Bun' is updated to 'Runtime: Node.js 20+ (production), Bun (development)'; the 'Bun-First Convention' section is rewritten to describe the runtime compatibility layer; commands section clarifies which require Bun vs Node.js

All CLAUDE.md changes are factually accurate and match the new code
bun run build passes

bun run knip passes

userStories.notes:
CLAUDE.md files to update:

   - Lines 53, 60: Keep 'bun workspaces' references since the monorepo still uses Bun workspaces for development
   - Lines 88-126: Commands section — add a note that `bun run <script>` commands are for development; production users interact via `opentabs` CLI (which runs on Node.js)
   - Lines 279-302: Rewrite 'Bun-First Convention' section. New title: 'Runtime Compatibility'. Explain the three tiers:

2. platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md:
   - Line 50: Update dev mode description
   - Lines 54-56: Clarify that bun --hot is dev-only; production uses Node.js
   - Add a note that the server runs on both Node.js and Bun

3. platform/cli/CLAUDE.md:
   - Add a note that the CLI runs on Node.js (installed via npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli)

4. platform/plugin-tools/CLAUDE.md:
   - Add note about Node.js compatibility

5. platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md:

6. plugins/CLAUDE.md:
   - Change all 'bun run build' to 'npm run build', 'bun install' to 'npm install'

7. e2e/CLAUDE.md:
   - Keep as-is (E2E tests run under Playwright/Node.js but the test server fixture spawns Bun)
   - Maybe add a clarifying note that the MCP server in E2E tests still uses Bun for hot-reload testing

8. .ralph/CLAUDE.md:
   - Line 16: 'bun install' → 'npm install' or keep if ralph workers use Bun
   - Actually, ralph workers run in the monorepo context where Bun is available, so keep as-is

userStories.title: Update README.md files to reflect Node.js-first user experience
userStories.description: As a potential user reading the README, I want to see that OpenTabs works with Node.js and npm so that I'm not deterred by a Bun requirement.
acceptanceCriteria: Root README.md: 'For Users' section lists Node.js 20+ as the requirement (not Bun); install instructions use npm
Root README.md: 'For Plugin Developers' section lists Node.js as required, not Bun; commands use npm/npx
Root README.md: 'For Platform Contributors' section lists Bun as additionally required
Root README.md: Command table uses npm commands for user/plugin-dev rows, bun commands for contributor rows
plugins/README.md: Changes bun commands to npm commands
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to update:

1. Root /README.md:
   - Line 102: 'You need [Bun](https://bun.sh/) (v1.0+)' → 'You need [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) (v20+)'
   - Lines 109, 115: 'bun install'/'bun run build' → 'npm install'/'npm run build'
   - Line 132: 'You need [Bun](https://bun.sh/) (>= 1.3.9) and Google Chrome' → 'You need [Bun](https://bun.sh/) (>= 1.3.9), [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) (v20+), and Google Chrome'
   - Lines 139-140: Keep 'bun install'/'bun run build' for contributor section (they use Bun)
   - Lines 153-162: Command table — update appropriately

2. plugins/README.md:
   - Line 7: 'bun run build', 'bun run lint' → 'npm run build', 'npm run lint'
   - Lines 11-15: bun commands → npm commands
   - Lines 23-24: 'bun run build:plugins'/'bun run check:plugins' — these are root monorepo commands (contributor context), keep as bun
   - Line 31: 'bun run check' → 'npm run check'
   - Line 37: 'bunx create-opentabs-plugin' → 'npx create-opentabs-plugin'

userStories.title: Update user-facing documentation (docs/) to reflect Node.js-first approach
userStories.description: As a user reading the docs site, I want installation guides and tutorials to show npm/Node.js commands so that I know I can use OpenTabs without Bun.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx: 'For Users' section requires Node.js not Bun; 'For Plugin Developers' requires Node.js not Bun; 'For Platform Contributors' requires Bun additionally; commands updated accordingly
docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx: Prerequisites list Node.js not Bun; commands use npm not bun
docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx: Commands use npx/npm instead of bunx/bun
docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx: Doctor check description updated; bunx → npx
docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx: Bun references updated to Node.js/npm where user-facing
docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx: bun --hot section is scoped to contributors only
docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx: Doctor output example updated
docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx: Keeps Bun requirement (contributor-only doc)
docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx: Updated to describe dual-runtime support
docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx: Keeps bun commands (contributor-only)
docs/components/illustrations.tsx: SVG text labels updated where they reference Bun in user-facing contexts
docs/components/ComponentInstall.tsx: The npm tab is the default; bun tab is secondary
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes:
This story targets the docs/ subproject. There are many files to update.

User-facing docs (change bun → npm/node):

1. docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx:
   - Line 84: Remove Bun requirement for plugin devs; add Node.js 20+ requirement
   - Lines 96, 102, 112: bun → npm commands
   - Line 115: Update prose about bun run build
   - Line 123: Keep Bun for contributors, but add it as an additional requirement
   - Lines 130-131: Keep bun for contributor section
   - Lines 143, 146: bun --hot section — scope to contributors only or move under contributor heading
   - Lines 161-166: Quality check commands — these are contributor commands, keep as bun

2. docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx:
   - Line 14: '[Bun](https://bun.sh) installed' → '[Node.js](https://nodejs.org) v20+ installed'
   - Line 21: 'bun install' → 'npm install'
   - Line 78: 'bun run build' → 'npm run build'
   - Line 99: 'bun run build compiled...' → 'npm run build compiled...'

3. docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx:
   - Line 14: 'bunx create-opentabs-plugin' → 'npx create-opentabs-plugin'
   - Line 16: 'bun install' → 'npm install'
   - Lines 398, 420, 469: 'bun run build/dev' → 'npm run build/dev'

4. docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx:
   - Line 228: 'Bun runtime — verifies Bun is installed' → 'Runtime — verifies Node.js is installed'
   - Line 387: 'bunx create-opentabs-plugin' → 'npx create-opentabs-plugin'

5. docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx:
   - Line 12: 'checks Bun, config...' → 'checks runtime, config...'
   - Line 134: 'After bun run build' → 'After npm run build' (or keep generic)
   - Line 166: 'Run bun run type-check' → 'Run npm run type-check'
   - Line 181: 'Run bun install' → 'Run npm install'
   - Line 200: 'Bun, config, server...' → 'Runtime, config, server...'

6. docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx:
   - Lines 301, 306: bun --hot section — add a note that this is contributor/dev-only

7. docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx:
   - Line 211: '✓ Bun runtime: v1.2.0' → '✓ Node.js runtime: v20.x.x' (or show both)
   - Line 218: 'bun run build' → 'npm run build'
   - Line 225: 'Bun runtime — is Bun installed?' → 'Runtime — is Node.js installed?'

Contributor-only docs (keep bun, maybe add context):

8. docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx: Keep Bun. Add a note at top that contributors need Bun in addition to Node.js.

9. docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx:
   - Line 13: 'It is a Bun.serve() HTTP + WebSocket server' → 'It is an HTTP + WebSocket server (using Bun.serve() in dev mode, node:http + ws in production)'
   - Lines 133, 137: Scope bun --hot description to dev mode

10. docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx: Keep bun commands (contributor-only)

11. docs/components/illustrations.tsx:
    - Line 890: 'Core platform packages (bun workspaces)' — keep or change to 'Core platform packages (workspaces)'
    - Line 1127: 'platform/ linked via Bun workspaces' → 'platform/ linked via workspaces'
    - Line 2690: '+ Bun runtime' → '+ Node.js 20+' in the user/plugin-dev install path; keep Bun for contributor path
    - Line 2747: 'git clone + bun install' → 'git clone + npm install' for user path
    - Line 2757, 2995: 'bun run build' — context-dependent

12. docs/components/ComponentInstall.tsx:
    - Make npm the default/first tab instead of bun
    - Line 61: Consider renaming or reordering tabs so npm is first

NOTE: This is a docs-only story. Use the docs project quality checks:
- cd docs && bun run build
- cd docs && bun run type-check  
- cd docs && bun run lint

The docs site itself still builds with bun (that's contributor tooling). Only the CONTENT changes to reflect the Node.js-first user experience.

userStories.title: Update error messages, help text, and CLI output across the codebase
userStories.description: As a normal user, I want all error messages and help text to reference npm/node commands instead of bun commands so that I can follow the instructions without confusion.
acceptanceCriteria: All user-facing error messages that say 'bun run build', 'bun install', or 'bun' commands are updated to use npm/node equivalents
The scaffold README template in scaffold.ts uses npm commands
The scaffold console output uses npm commands
The doctor command output shows 'Node.js runtime' instead of 'Bun runtime' when running on Node.js
The resolver.ts log messages about 'bun pm -g bin' are updated to be more generic
No user-facing string in the CLI, MCP server, or plugin-tools references 'bun' as a requirement
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the cleanup sweep for any remaining user-facing bun references that weren't caught in earlier stories.

Do a thorough search across all platform/ source files (excluding test files) for:
- Strings containing 'bun run'
- Strings containing 'bun install'
- Strings containing 'bunx'
- Strings containing 'Bun runtime'
- Error messages mentioning 'bun'
- console.log/console.error containing 'bun'

Key locations to double-check:
- platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts line 181: 'bun pm -g bin failed' — this is a debug log about discovering bun's global packages. Keep it (it's factual — bun's global path discovery may fail), but make sure it only runs when Bun is available.
- platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts line 219: 'PORT=<number> bun dev' — should be generic
- platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts line 135: Already updated in US-004
- platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts line 103: Already updated in US-004
- e2e/global-setup.ts line 65: 'Run: cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install && bun run build' — this is E2E (contributor only), keep

Also check:
- platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts — error messages about rebuilding plugins
- platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts — generated file content

This story is marked as e2eCheckpoint: true because it's the final code story and E2E tests should verify the full system works end-to-end.
